## What landed — [GQ2/ShapiroRead.lean](GQ2/ShapiroRead.lean) (new, ~460 lines, std-3 exactly)

**The keystone dissolved.** The scoping docs feared "Shapiro's H¹ iso built from scratch"; it reduced to one explicit-witness identity. For a 1-cocycle `β : G → 𝔽₂[G/N]` (mk′-action), with scalar read `α(n) := β(n)(1̄)` and primitive `w(u) := β(ũ)(u)`:

$$\mathrm{Sh}(\alpha)(g) = \beta(g) + \big(\mathrm{mk}'(g)\cdot w - w\big)$$

proved by evaluating the cocycle rule on the two factorizations of `g·(g⁻¹•u)~ = ũ·ℓ_u(g)` at the coset `u` — no choices, no H¹-machinery, no transversal-Λ construction.

**Deliverables** (all `#print axioms` = std-3, `lake build` green, census 15):
- `hcoh_square` / `hcoh_free` / `hcoh_involution` — the three per-orbit `hcoh` producers, matching `lemma_6_15_*` **verbatim** at the block coordinates (B²-shift via banked `graphPullback_sub_mem_B2` at the block-supported primitive, uniform in the same-block `j = k` free case; new `graphPullback_comap` functoriality)
- `phiRes_evalW` — the deep-coordinate identification is a **`rfl`** — plus `shapiroCoord_mem_deepClasses` (the f2d `hvanish` feed) and the `Z¹`-package `shapiroCoord_mem_Z1`

## Coordination with the live swarm

I staged only my hunks — `GQ2.lean` was staged as an index blob carrying just my import line, leaving the other agent's uncommitted `MStageCount` registration intact in the working tree. My board-row update rides the working tree for the swarm's next board commit; the [p15f2-handoff](docs/p15f2-handoff.md) is updated and committed (f2c1 §5 rewritten as a landed record + f2d consumption recipe, plus a §9 session block with three new Lean gotchas banked — including the `rw`-under-unreduced-pair-projection metavariable capture).

**P-15f2 remaining**: c2a ∥ c2b (involution Kummer field data — the one open mathematical input, claimed by the parallel agent), then **f2d** (thin composition + the SectionSix statement-move, coordinated with P-15f1/f8). ## What's genuinely available (unstaffed + dependency-ready)

**⭐ Best pick — P-15f8** (`lemma_6_17_dim`, the §6.3 dimension core — a top-level sorry). Both halves are already built and on `master`:
- `DeepCount.hduality_of_data` (f7's output) produces `hduality`;
- `DimAssembly.lemma_6_17_dim_of_hduality` consumes exactly that shape and produces `#deepPart² = #H¹`.

The output/input shapes match verbatim. **Alternatives (both ⭐⭐⭐, design-heavy, in the P-16 §8 lane):**
- **P-16d6e3** — the local (140) residues (`hpartial_local`/`hsep`/`hZcard` in `Phase140Local.lean`). This is the one sorry currently *failing the gate*. Deepish: needs `prop_5_16` cup-duality clauses.

**My recommendation: P-15f8.** It's the most self-contained available work (both halves exist, it's mostly plumbing named producers), and it closes a top-level sorry — collapsing the §6 lane to just the vanish clause your other agents are already finishing.

## What I found

The prior (idle) P-15f8 session had **already proved the entire thing** and committed it — but left it **orphaned**: `GQ2/DimClose.lean` and `GQ2/ResidueLift.lean` were sorry-free and committed, yet **not imported from `GQ2.lean`**, so the assembly of `lemma_6_17_dim` wasn't even in the library build (invisible to CI, prone to silent bitrot).

`ResidueLift.lemma_6_17_dim_final` proves `#X₊² = #H¹(ℚ₂,V)` from **exactly** `lemma_6_17_dim`'s own hypotheses — genuinely unconditional:
- the `[Finite (H¹(ker ρ,𝔽₂))]` instance is discharged internally (`finite_H1_open`, since `ker ρ` is open);
- the one "arithmetic input" — a residue-trivial lift of tame inertia — is **proved from the existing budget**: the tame relation `σ⁻¹τσ = τ²` makes `τ` a commutator, and commutators are residue-trivial via Teichmüller approximation (`exists_rootOfUnity_near`, off the B13 filtration) + commuting Galois action on roots of unity. No residue-field machinery, no new axiom.

## What I did

1. **Registered** `DimClose` + `ResidueLift` in [GQ2.lean](GQ2.lean) — the proof is now part of the build.
2. **Verified**: `lake build GQ2.ResidueLift` green (8654 jobs); `#print axioms lemma_6_17_dim_final` = **std-3 + {B6, B7, B11a, B12, B13}** — the §6.3 deep-part budget, **no new axiom**.
3. **Committed** ([ae8fd55](GQ2.lean)) just the 2-line import registration, staged as an index blob so the concurrent swarm edits to `GQ2.lean` stay intact. Board row + splice recipe recorded in [tickets.md](docs/tickets.md).

## What remains — and why I stopped here

That's **co-owned with the active P-15f2d agent** — `GQ2/VanishClose.lean` is in the tree but only has wiring bricks so far, not the vanish-final. Doing the SectionSix/`prop_6_18` edit now would collide head-on and can't even typecheck (no downstream vanish proof yet). So the dim proof is staged and ready; the joint move should happen once f2d's vanish lands. I've left the exact recipe on the board and did **not** touch SectionSix.

**Net:** P-15f8's mathematics is done, verified, and now actually in the build; `lemma_6_17_dim` collapses to a one-shot splice that's gated on f2d. Want me to pick up one of the P-16 residue bricks (e3 or e4a) while that unblocks, or prepare the joint splice leaf as a skeleton so it's ready the moment f2d's vanish proof lands?

I've hit the key structural issue the handoff flagged: `W = Fin K → RegRep N` gets the **global trivial `AbsGalQ2`-action** (lifted pointwise from `ZMod 2` via `Pi`), which beats a proof-local `letI` of the intended `mk'`-action — so the reducer would use the wrong action. The dim side (f8) dodged this with an instance-opaque `RegMod` synonym. Let me check that pattern and whether f2b's `W` needs the same wrapper.

Critical insight to verify: `RegRep N` is itself an **opaque `def`** (`OrbitData.lean:97`), unlike `RegMod` whose innermost `C → ZMod 2` is transparent. If `RegRep N`'s opacity blocks the global trivial `ZMod 2`-action from lifting, then `DistribMulAction AbsGalQ2 (Fin K → RegRep N)` is *unresolved* by default and I can `letI` the correct action cleanly — making the assembly far more tractable. Let me test this empirically.

**Confirmed** — both `DistribMulAction AbsGalQ2 (RegRep N)` and `DistribMulAction AbsGalQ2 (Fin K → RegRep N)` **fail to synthesize** by default. `RegRep N`'s opacity blocks the global trivial action (unlike `RegMod`), so I can `letI` the correct `mk'`-action cleanly — **no synonym needed**. This substantially de-risks the assembly. The handoff's warning was `RegMod`-specific.

## The verdict: the frozen package shape is REFUTED

The `TamePackage{Unram,Ram}`/`hpack` witnesses (`ρ.1.1 = c ∘ tameA` in `RF.YC`) **cannot be derived — they're unsatisfiable at general frames**. The kill shot is the boundary equation's θ-component: it pins `θ_C(ρ(x₀)) = ψ̄(piX0)`, while any tame factorization forces that value to be 1 — and `thm_4_2` quantifies over *all* frames, including ones with `ψ̄(piX0) ≠ 1` and nonempty lifts. Independently, local `hfaith` is false whenever `K < L_Y` (same structural point as the e6/e7 amendment). The paper never claims either — §6 explicitly runs over the faithful image `H_V` (p. 25).

## The reshape: head-inflation, and it's *cheaper* than the packages

The escape is lovely: the boundary equation's **head** component literally *is* the tame factorization — `TC.piY ∘ ρ.1.1 = α ∘ tameA/tameF`, `rfl`-deep, for every lift of both sources. So instead of per-ρ packages, make the enrichment's κ⁰ datum definitionally head-inflated and push every evaluation down `graphPullback_reindexHom` to the faithful head quotient, where `hfaith` is true by construction, wild slots are literally 1, and the dichotomy is a `by_cases`. **Every package field dissolves — the G0-obtain becomes hypothesis-free.** All in [p16d6e4aA-p4-tame-package.md](docs/p16d6e4aA-p4-tame-package.md), with the reuse map (peels, counts, transports — all banked) and P3's interface untouched.
